trip_submit_failed.title: Trip Submit Failed
rescue_guide.title: Animal Rescue Guide
rescue_guide.subtitle: How to Help Stray Animals — A Step-by-Step Guide
rescue_guide.safety.title: Safety First
rescue_guide.safety.description: Before attempting any rescue, ensure your own safety and that of the animal. Do not corner or chase frightened animals.
rescue_guide.assess.title: Assess the Situation
rescue_guide.assess.description: Observe the animal from a safe distance. Check if it is injured, trapped, or in immediate danger. Note its location and condition.
rescue_guide.approach.title: Approach Carefully
rescue_guide.approach.description: Move slowly and speak softly. Offer food (water and a small amount of plain food) to build trust. Give the animal space.
rescue_guide.contain.title: Contain Safely
rescue_guide.contain.description: Use a humane trap, carrier, or sturdy box with a soft cloth. Never grab an unknown animal directly — use gloves and a towel.
rescue_guide.transport.title: Transport to Safety
rescue_guide.transport.description: Keep the animal in a warm, dark, quiet space during transport. Do not feed unless directed by a veterinarian.
rescue_guide.support.title: Seek Professional Support
rescue_guide.support.description: Contact a local shelter, rescue organization, or veterinarian immediately. Report the rescue through PawHaven's Report Stray feature.
rescue_guide.emergency.title: Emergency Contacts
rescue_guide.footer.disclaimer: This guide is for informational purposes only. Always consult a professional for medical or behavioral concerns.
